title: BAI President Addresses India Open 'Monkey' Incident
indian_angle: The President of the Badminton Association of India, Himanta Biswa Sarma, commented on the unusual presence of monkeys during matches at the India Open.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma made a light-hearted remark regarding the presence of monkeys at the India Open, asking 'What is the problem if monkeys come to watch a game?'.

title: Indian Shuttlers Urged to Develop Independent Court Thinking
indian_angle: This opinion piece highlights the crucial need for Indian badminton players to cultivate self-reliance and strategic thinking on court, reducing their dependence on coaches.
summary: An article emphasizes that Indian shuttlers should learn to think on their feet and secure victories without constant coaching, fostering greater independence in their game.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System for Indian Junior Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India is implementing a new 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 categories.
summary: The BAI has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for domestic ranking tournaments and national championships for junior categories, set to debut in Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Reaches US Open Final, Finishes Runner-Up
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth made a strong run to the final of the US Open before ultimately finishing as the runner-up.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth's campaign at the US Open concluded with a loss in the final match, preventing him from claiming the title.

title: Indian Shuttler Rounak Chouhan Stuns World No 6 at US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan delivered a significant upset by defeating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open.
summary: Rounak Chouhan, an Indian shuttler, made headlines at the US Open by impressively beating the highly-ranked Chou Tien Chen.

title: India Seeks to Host BWF World Championships 2026
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has issued an Expression of Interest to potentially host the prestigious BWF World Championships in 2026.
summary: BAI has invited Expressions of Interest for organizing the BWF World Championships 2026, indicating India's ambition to host a major global badminton event.
